name: Cathedral of Saint Joseph Food Pantry
description: Weekly food pantry providing supplemental groceries, clothing, and housewares to Hartford's Asylum Hill community at no cost. Located in the Cathedral Community Center behind the Archdiocese of Hartford building.
howToApply: Walk in during pantry hours. Bring photo ID.

name: South Church - Feed the HART Food Pantry
description: Community food pantry serving downtown Hartford residents. Provides groceries and basic food items to individuals and families in need.
howToApply: Walk in during pantry hours at the south end of the building.

name: St. Patrick-St. Anthony Franciscan Center - Sandwich Ministry
organization: St. Patrick-St. Anthony Church / Franciscan Center for Urban Ministry
description: Daily sandwich ministry serving 100-120 people per day since the early 1990s. Distributes sandwiches, water, and fresh baked goods to anyone in need regardless of race or faith. 50 volunteer teams prepare and distribute meals year-round.
howToApply: Walk up during distribution hours. No ID or documentation required.

name: Saint Ann-Immaculate Conception Church Food Pantry
description: Church-based food pantry serving residents of the Frog Hollow neighborhood. Distributes groceries to families in need twice weekly.
howToApply: Walk in during pantry hours. Bring proof of residency and ID.

name: Loaves and Fishes Ministries - Soup Kitchen
description: Daily soup kitchen in the Asylum Hill neighborhood providing hot meals to Hartford residents experiencing food insecurity. Serves thousands of unduplicated individuals annually, including meals-to-go and pantry bags.
howToApply: Walk in during meal service hours. No ID or documentation required.

name: House of Bread Soup Kitchen
description: Community soup kitchen providing free hot meals to people in need from the Hartford area. Operates Monday through Friday out of a location on Chestnut Street, serving breakfast and lunch.
howToApply: Walk in during meal service hours. Open to all.

name: Asylum Hill Congregational Church - Community Meal
description: Weekly community meal serving approximately 150 meals every Sunday afternoon to neighbors in need. Also hosts Loaves and Fishes Ministries soup kitchen program during the week.
howToApply: Walk in during meal service. No registration or ID required.

name: Connecticut Foodshare - Mobile Food Pantry (Hartford)
description: Mobile outdoor food distribution for low-income Hartford residents at rotating locations. Provides fresh produce, bread, and other groceries. Multiple distribution sites across Hartford neighborhoods.
hours: Varies by location - check website or text FOODSHARE to 85511
howToApply: Show up at distribution site. No registration needed. Text FOODSHARE to 85511 for schedule updates.

name: ImmaCare - Emergency Shelter for Men
description: 24-hour emergency shelter for men who are most vulnerable to life on the streets, including those with debilitating health issues, severe substance abuse addictions, and mental health issues. Provides safe beds, meals, clothing, showers, laundry, and comprehensive case management. Operating since 1981.
howToApply: Walk in or call 211 for referral. Intake available 24/7.

name: My Sisters' Place - Women's Housing Services
description: Nonprofit committed to ending homelessness by empowering women, families, and others in Greater Hartford. Provides transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and wraparound services to help residents achieve independence and stability.
howToApply: Visit website or contact through sistersplacect.org for intake process.

name: Habitat for Humanity of North Central Connecticut
organization: Habitat for Humanity of North Central CT
description: Builds and repairs homes for low-income families in Greater Hartford. Offers new construction, rehab projects, existing homeowner repair programs, and financial literacy education. Also operates ReStore thrift stores.
hours: Mon-Fri office hours; check website for project schedules
howToApply: Apply online at hfhncc.org or call to learn about homeowner programs and volunteer opportunities.

name: Rebuilding Together Hartford - Free Home Repair
description: Provides free home repairs to low-income homeowners in Hartford including emergency repairs for heat, hot water, plumbing, electrical, and equipment to aid in daily living. Helps people live in warmth, safety, and independence.
howToApply: Apply online at rthartford.org/apply-for-services or call. Must be a Hartford homeowner with low income.

name: Salvation Army - Adult Rehabilitation Center
description: Free 180-day residential rehabilitation program for adults struggling with addiction. Combines group and individual therapy, life skills training, recreational activities, work therapy, and spiritual support. Provides housing, meals, and clothing during the program.
howToApply: Call (800) 728-7825 or (860) 527-8106 to begin the intake process. No fee for the program.

name: CCAR Hartford Recovery Community Center
description: Free addiction recovery support center offering peer recovery coaching, telephone recovery support, virtual support meetings, and connections to area support services. Helps people at all stages of recovery navigate the recovery community.
howToApply: Walk in or call. All services are free. No insurance needed.

name: The Hartford House - Men's Sober Living
description: Structured sober living home for men in recovery from drug and alcohol addiction. Provides private bedrooms with internet and TV, on-site house manager, regular checks, and compassionate accountability in a substance-free environment.
howToApply: Call or email jeffcmcdonough@gmail.com. Private pay - contact for rates.

name: Oxford House Sherwood - Men's Sober Living
description: Self-run, self-supported recovery house for men recovering from alcoholism and drug addiction. Democratically operated by residents with no time limit on stay. Part of the national Oxford House network with over 4,000 locations.
howToApply: Call the house directly. Must interview with current residents and be voted in. Self-pay rent.

name: Institute of Living - Addiction Services
description: Comprehensive addiction treatment through Hartford HealthCare's behavioral health network. Offers residential, day treatment, intensive outpatient (IOP), and standard outpatient tracks. All tracks offer medication-assisted treatment (MAT) including Suboxone, methadone, and Vivitrol.
howToApply: Call main line at (860) 545-7000 or outpatient clinic at (860) 545-7330 for intake assessment.

name: The Network - Domestic Violence Services
description: 24/7 domestic violence services including a short-term 15-bed confidential safe house, short-term counseling, support programs, court advocacy, and online resources. Serves individuals and families fleeing domestic violence.
howToApply: Call hotline at (860) 763-4542 anytime. All services are confidential.

name: Connecticut Fair Housing Center
description: Free legal services for housing discrimination cases. Sends testers to collect evidence of discrimination, helps file complaints with state and federal agencies. Handles cases involving race, disability, family status, source of income, and other protected classes.
howToApply: Call (860) 247-4400 or toll-free (888) 247-4401 to report discrimination. Can also file online.

name: Community Partners in Action - Reentry Welcome Center
description: Centralized hub for formerly incarcerated individuals returning to Hartford. Provides basic needs assistance, housing support, substance use and mental health treatment referrals, job readiness training, career development, and peer support groups. Has served over 4,000 individuals.
howToApply: Walk in during business hours or call. Contact Anthony Carter at (860) 973-2748 ext. 2748 for information.

name: Our Piece of the Pie (OPP) - Youth Employment & Education
organization: Our Piece of the Pie, Inc.
description: Empowers youth ages 14-24 in Greater Hartford to succeed in education, employment, and life. Each youth is paired with a Youth Development Specialist to create an individualized success plan. Services include academic support, job readiness, internships, job placement, and post-secondary preparation.
howToApply: Call or visit website. Serves youth with risk factors including under-credited, child welfare involved, justice involved, or low-income and unemployed.

name: ConnectiKids - Youth Tutoring & Mentoring
description: Nonprofit positive youth development organization providing one-to-one after-school tutoring and mentoring to Hartford youth. Focuses on literacy, educational support, socio-emotional development, and career orientation. Operates at 11 locations across Hartford.
hours: After-school hours; contact for specific site schedules
howToApply: Call or email ctkids@ct-kids.org to connect with a mentoring site near you.

name: Nutmeg Big Brothers Big Sisters
organization: Big Brothers Big Sisters of Connecticut
description: Connecticut's largest volunteer mentoring network, making professionally supported matches between adult volunteers and children ages 6-18. Provides community-based and school-based mentoring across 111 cities and towns.
howToApply: Call or visit ctbigs.org to enroll a child (Little) or sign up as a mentor (Big).

name: Hartford Communities That Care - Violence Intervention
description: Community-based violence prevention since 1998. Operates Connecticut's first Hospital-Linked Violence Intervention Program (HVIP) with 24/7 crisis response for crime victims. Programs include mental health support, workforce readiness, youth leadership academy, and family wellness.
howToApply: Visit website at hartfordctc.org or contact through their online form. Crisis response available 24/7.

name: Bridge Family Center - Youth & Family Counseling
description: Nonprofit providing counseling, crisis intervention, mentoring, and life-skills training for high-risk youth, teens, young adults, families, and caregivers in Greater Hartford. Services include individual and family counseling, residential programs, and support groups.
howToApply: Call (860) 313-1119 for counseling intake. Crisis services may also be available by calling the main number.

name: Urban League of Greater Hartford
description: Promotes racial equity through programs in adult education, youth development, workforce development and training, economic empowerment, and health and wellness. Serves over 3,000 individuals and families annually since 1964.
howToApply: Call or visit to learn about available programs and registration.

name: CRT Meals on Wheels - Senior Nutrition
description: Home-delivered meals program providing two meals per day, five days a week, year-round to homebound elderly residents in Greater Hartford. Hot lunches delivered between 10am-1pm. Accommodates special diets. $3 donation suggested but no one denied a meal.
howToApply: Call (860) 560-5848. Referrals accepted from doctors, hospitals, VNAs, and town social services. Assessment interview required.

name: Catholic Charities - Hispanic Senior Center
description: Senior center at the Institute for the Hispanic Family offering enriching programs and services for individuals aged 55 and older. Includes socialization, recreational activities, learning experiences, and health and wellness programming with bilingual Spanish/English services.
howToApply: Call or visit during business hours. Open to individuals aged 55+.

name: Catholic Charities - South End Wellness Senior Center
description: Senior wellness center in Hartford's South End neighborhood providing social programs, wellness activities, nutrition support, and community connections for older adults. Culturally and linguistically competent services.
howToApply: Call or visit during business hours.

name: Hartford WIC Program - Main Office
organization: City of Hartford Health & Human Services
description: Women, Infants, and Children (WIC) program providing nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy food benefits, and referrals to healthcare and social services for pregnant women, new mothers, infants, and children up to age 5.
howToApply: Call (860) 757-4780 to schedule an appointment. Bring proof of income, ID, and proof of address.

name: Hartford WIC Program - Santa Marquez Center
organization: City of Hartford Health & Human Services
description: Second WIC office in downtown Hartford providing nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy food benefits, and referrals. Includes Saturday hours twice monthly for working families.
hours: Mon, Wed, Thurs, Fri: 8am-5pm; Tues: 8am-6:30pm; 2 Saturdays/month: 8am-2pm
howToApply: Call (860) 757-4860 for appointment. Bring proof of income, ID, and proof of address.

name: Goodwin University Dental Hygiene Clinic
description: Free dental hygiene services provided by supervised dental hygiene students. Services include cleanings, x-rays, sealants, oral cancer screenings, root debridement, fluoride, whitening trays, occlusal guards, and nutritional counseling. Open to children and adults.
howToApply: Call (860) 218-1800 or fill out online appointment form. Appointments required.

name: Vision To Learn - Hartford
description: Free vision screenings, eye exams, and prescription glasses for students in underserved Hartford communities. Mobile clinics visit Hartford Public Schools and Hartford Public Library branches. Partners with local Boys & Girls Clubs.
address: Hartford Public Schools and Library locations
howToApply: Email connecticut@visiontolearn.org or ask at your child's Hartford Public School. Services provided through schools and partner sites.

name: YWCA Hartford Region - Programs & Services
description: Multi-service nonprofit providing early childhood education (Early Learning Centers), financial literacy programs, VITA free tax preparation, career training for youth, and advocacy. Hosts annual Denise L. Nappier Money Conference for working families.
hours: Early Learning Centers: Mon-Fri 7:00am-5:30pm; office hours vary
howToApply: Call or visit website for specific program registration information.

name: CRT VITA Free Tax Preparation
description: Free federal and state tax preparation and filing for households earning up to $69,000/year. Trained volunteers help prepare and file basic tax returns at no cost. IRS refunds issued via direct deposit.
howToApply: Call 211, press 3, then 6 to schedule. Or visit 211ct.org/taxhelp. Bring W-2s, ID, Social Security cards, and prior year return.

name: KNOX - Community Gardens & Urban Farming
description: Hartford's premier urban greening organization managing 21 community gardens across 10 low-income neighborhoods, providing 250+ families access to green space and fresh food. Also offers urban farming training program, youth environmental education, and green job skills for young adults.
howToApply: Call or visit website to apply for a garden plot or enroll in farming/job training programs.

name: Hartford Superior Court - Housing Court
description: State court handling housing-related cases including evictions, housing code violations, and landlord-tenant disputes. Self-help resources available for people without attorneys.
howToApply: Visit during business hours. If facing eviction, call Eviction Help CT at 1-800-559-1565 for free legal representation.

name: InterCommunity - Recovery House (Residential)
description: Recovery house program providing safe, substance-free housing with 30 hours/week of group and individual therapy, psychiatric and nursing support, and peer support. Also operates a 12-bed Intermediate Enhanced long-term residential program for co-occurring mental health and substance use disorders.
howToApply: Call InterCommunity main line or visit website for referral and intake process.
